collections.title: All Home Duas — One PDF
collections.description: All six daily prayers for the home — entering, leaving, meals, waking, bathroom, and protection — in a single print-ready A4 PDF with a placement guide included.

duas.title: Dua for Entering the Home
duas.summary: Recited upon stepping into your home, this prayer invites Allah's blessing into the household and ensures Shaytan cannot enter with you or share in your evening meal.
duas.placement: Inside your front door, at eye level
duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَلَجْنَا، وَبِسْمِ اللَّهِ خَرَجْنَا، وَعَلَى رَبِّنَا تَوَكَّلْنَا
duas.transliteration: Bismillāhi walajnā, wa bismillāhi kharajnā, wa ʿalā Rabbinā tawakkalnā
duas.translation: In the name of Allah we enter, in the name of Allah we leave, and upon our Lord we place our trust.
duas.virtues: The Prophet ﷺ would say this dua upon entering the home. It establishes barakah in the household and reminds the family that their dwelling is under Allah's protection.

duas.title: Dua for Leaving the Home
duas.summary: Said before stepping out, this prayer draws Allah's guidance, protection, and sufficiency. The Prophet ﷺ taught that whoever recites it will be told: "You are guided, protected, and kept safe."
duas.placement: Inside your front door, paired with the Entering prayer
duas.arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ، وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
duas.transliteration: Bismillāh, tawakkaltu ʿala-llāh, wa lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata illā billāh
duas.translation: In the name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah, and there is no might nor power except with Allah.
duas.virtues: Upon leaving the home with this dua, an angel says: 'You are guided, defended, and protected.' Shaytan turns away from one who says it.

duas.title: Dua Before & After Meals
duas.summary: Two short prayers — one to begin a meal in Allah's name and invite His blessing, and one to end it with gratitude. This PDF includes both, designed to sit at the dining table or kitchen wall.
duas.virtues: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'When one of you eats food, let him say Bismillah. If he forgets at the beginning, let him say: Bismillahi fi awwalihi wa akhirihi.'

duas.title: Dua Upon Waking
duas.summary: The first words of the day — an acknowledgement that sleep is a small death and each morning is a renewed gift from Allah. A physical reminder by the bed makes this Sunnah effortless to build as a daily habit.
duas.placement: Bedside table or the wall you face upon waking
duas.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
duas.transliteration: Alḥamdu lillāhi-lladhī aḥyānā baʿda mā amātanā wa ilayhi-n-nushūr
duas.translation: All praise is for Allah who gave us life after having taken it from us, and unto Him is the resurrection.
duas.virtues: The Prophet ﷺ recited this upon waking. Beginning the day with gratitude to Allah sets the spiritual tone for every hour that follows.

duas.title: Dua Before Entering the Bathroom
duas.summary: Seeking refuge from the evil spirits that dwell in unclean places, this prayer is recited just before crossing the threshold — never inside. Place it where it's seen every single time.
duas.placement: Just outside the bathroom door, at eye level
duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْخُبُثِ وَالْخَبَائِثِ
duas.transliteration: Allāhumma innī aʿūdhu bika mina-l-khubthi wa-l-khabāʾith
duas.translation: O Allah, I seek refuge with You from the male and female evil spirits.
duas.virtues: The Prophet ﷺ said to seek this protection before entering the bathroom. It shields the person from the jinn that inhabit unclean places.

duas.title: Dua Against the Evil Eye
duas.summary: A comprehensive supplication seeking refuge in Allah's perfect words from all harm. The Prophet ﷺ used this prayer to protect his grandsons Hasan and Husayn — particularly powerful for children's rooms.
duas.arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّةِ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطَانٍ وَهَامَّةٍ، وَمِنْ كُلِّ عَيْنٍ لَامَّةٍ
duas.transliteration: Aʿūdhu bi-kalimāti-llāhi-t-tāmmati min kulli shayṭānin wa hāmmah, wa min kulli ʿaynin lāmmah
duas.translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from every devil and every poisonous creature, and from every evil eye.
duas.virtues: The Prophet ﷺ used to seek Allah's protection for al-Hasan and al-Husayn using these words, and say: 'Your forefather Ibrahim used to seek protection for Ismail and Ishaaq with these same words.'

duas.title: Umrah Step-by-Step Guide
duas.summary: A complete A4 printable guide to performing Umrah — every step clearly laid out with duas for each stage. Print, laminate, cut along the marks, bind with a ring, and wear during your journey to the blessed Haram.
duas.placement: Print · laminate · cut · bind with a ring · hang around neck during Umrah
duas.virtues: A comprehensive reference to carry during Umrah so every step — from entering Ihram to performing Tawaf, Sa'i, and shaving — is done with confidence and the correct supplications.

comingSoon.title: Dua on the Go
comingSoon.description: Printable strip cards for travel — journey prayers, marketplace duas, mosque duas, and key Dhikr for the car. Designed to laminate and carry. Front: dua in Arabic, transliteration, translation. Back: the full Hadith.
comingSoon.notifySubject: Notify me: Dua on the Go

comingSoon.title: Allah's 99 Names — Small Booklet
comingSoon.description: A small, pocketable booklet — each page one Name of Allah in Arabic, transliteration, and meaning. Colour-coded by category (Mercy, Majesty, Wisdom…). One A4 sheet yields 18 booklet pages when folded and trimmed.
